primary_pair_metric: trap crash count (single __builtin_trap site reached after passing all CHAIN_DEPTH narrow CMPs)
expected_direction: vp >> naive at every CHAIN_DEPTH; vp/naive ratio MONOTONE NON-DECREASING in CHAIN_DEPTH

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile: linear time-to-trap in CHAIN_DEPTH (each CMP's CMP_MAP gradient walks independently to its TARGET byte; bytes already-aligned via prior gradient stay aligned because they're retained on the prior CMP's coverage edge)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.naive: geometric collapse: trap rate ≈ 1/256^K per attempt → 1/4 at K=2, ~10^-5 at K=4, unreachable at K=8 within 600s

seed_corpus.description: Single 16-byte zero seed (data[0..15] = 0x00). Matches CHAIN_DEPTH=16 max. Provides initial popcount of ~4 per byte (Hamming distance from 0x00 to TARGETS averages ~4 bits; e.g., 0x4D = 0100 1101 has popcount 4).
seed_corpus.rationale: Zero seed is a clean control: every TARGET byte starts at the same Hamming distance regardless of CHAIN_DEPTH. VP's gradient-walk distance is comparable across scan values.

acceptance.metric: median trap crash count, vp / naive ratio per scan
acceptance.rule: vp_median > 5 at EVERY CHAIN_DEPTH AND naive_median geometric (>10 at K=2, <2 at K=8) AND vp/naive ratio MONOTONE NON-DECREASING from K=2 to K=16

design_basis: v2 redesign after v1 refutation. v1 used a single 32-bit CMP with optional bijective derivation; refuted because single-CMP gradient retention adds at most ~33 buckets which is too few for VP to systematically beat naive given libafl's mostly-byte-level mutator schedule. v2 chains K narrow (1-byte) CMPs so VP gets K independent Hamming-distance gradients to walk; naive faces multiplicative 1/256^K cost. The mechanism is the SAME (CMP_MAP gradient retention) but compounds via the chain rather than depending on a single CMP's gradient. Same primary pair (value_profile, naive) and same axis_differ (CMP_MAP added to naive's coverage-only baseline).
notes: Strictly two fuzzers per template — only the primary pair (A=value_profile, B=naive) is verified here. The same harness would also show cmp >> naive (I2S substitutes each byte one-shot) and vpc ≈ cmp (vpc has cmp's I2S); those are essentially i2s_magic_number_gate with WIDTH=1 chained K times — already in the catalog under that template.

Falsifier: if vp/naive ratio is FLAT or DECAYS with CHAIN_DEPTH, VP's gradient is NOT compounding across CMPs in libafl's value_profile implementation. Possible diagnoses: (a) CMP_MAP retains FEWER buckets per CMP than documentation suggests; (b) libafl's mutator schedule produces too few single-bit flips for the gradient to walk effectively; (c) VP's per-edge retention isn't independently weighted in the scheduler. Either substantive diagnosis advances the methodology-limit understanding.
